name: Excessive DNS Queries to Multiple Distinct Domains (SIEM Correlation Needed)
description: Detects a single source IP querying an unusually high number of distinct domains (e.g., >50) within a short timeframe (e.g., 60 seconds). This behavior is highly indicative of potential DNS tunneling, C2, or rapid reconnaissance.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of one of the DNS query events (e.g., the most recent one).

parameters.name: SourceIp
parameters.description: The IP address of the client making the DNS query.

parameters.name: DestinationIp
parameters.description: The IP address of the DNS server being queried.

parameters.name: QueryType
parameters.description: The DNS query type (e.g., A, AAAA, TXT, CNAME).

parameters.name: ResponseCode
parameters.description: The DNS response code (e.g., NOERROR, NXDOMAIN).

parameters.name: ComputerName
parameters.description: The hostname of the source if available.

parameters.name: RuleName
parameters.description: The name of the Sigma rule that triggered the event, if applicable.

parameters.name: SIEM_COUNT_OF_DISTINCT_QUERIES
parameters.description: The aggregated count of distinct QueryNames by the SIEM (e.g., 65).

parameters.name: SIEM_TIMEFRAME
parameters.description: The time window over which the events were counted (e.g., '60 seconds').

parameters.name: SIEM_FIRST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the first event in the correlated group.

parameters.name: SIEM_LAST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last event in the correlated group.
